channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: World Cup Sparks Growth in Soccer Participation Across Northern Ontario
item.description: The article highlights how the World Cup has ignited renewed interest in soccer among youth in northern Ontario, leading to increased participation and local initiatives. While the main story focuses ...

item.title: Omtatah Calls for Clarity on 3-Year Delay in KEMRI Leadership Appointment
item.description: Busia Senator Okiya Omtatah has raised concerns about the prolonged vacancy at Kenya Medical Research Institute (KEMRI), which has operated without a substantive director-general since 2022. In a stat...

item.title: Health Impacts of Prolonged Fasting: A Scientific Overview
item.description: The article explores the physiological changes in the human body during prolonged fasting, citing studies that show significant shifts in energy sources and organ functions. It highlights weight loss,...

item.title: Burna Boy And Shakira Rehearse For FIFA World Cup Final Halftime Performance
item.description: Naija News reports that Nigerian Afrobeats superstar Burna Boy and Colombian pop icon Shakira have reunited ahead of Sunday’s historic FIFA World Cup final halftime show. The duo, who previously perfo...

item.title: Shark Attack Injures Scuba Diver Off Tasmania's Adventure Bay
item.description: A 31-year-old scuba diver was injured in a shark attack off the coast of Tasmania's Bruny Island, according to police reports. The incident occurred around 9:10 am on Saturday when the man, who was sp...

item.title: Labour Party Selects Andy Burnham as New Leader Following Leadership Contest
item.description: Veteran Labour Party politician Andy Burnham has been chosen as the new leader of the UK's governing party, becoming Britain's next prime minister without a general election. Burnham secured the neces...

item.title: Australian arrested at US airport for overstaying visa
item.description: Footage from a Las Vegas airport shows an Australian citizen being tackled and handcuffed by immigration officers after allegedly overstaying their visa. The incident, captured on camera, has sparked ...

item.title: R'Bonney Gabriel Reflects on Emotional Experience After 'Project Runway' Elimination
item.description: R'Bonney Gabriel, a contestant in the popular design competition 'Project Runway,' expressed deep frustration following her elimination from the show. In a candid interview, she shared her emotional s...

item.title: Pakistan's Strategic Moves to Strengthen US Security Ties and Minerals Investment
item.description: The article discusses Pakistan's recent decision to hire a US-based lobbying firm, Ervin Graves Strategy Group, to enhance bilateral security cooperation and attract minerals investment. The two-year,...

item.title: Donald Trump Criticizes England's Tactical Approach in World Cup Defeat
item.description: US President Donald Trump joined the chorus of critics targeting England's tactical decisions during their FIFA World Cup semi-final loss to Argentina. Speaking at a reception in New York alongside FI...

item.title: DNA Test Reveals 38-Year-Old Birth Switch Lawsuit Against North Dakota Hospital
item.description: Two men, Kyle Bylin and Jeremy Morrison, discovered through DNA tests that they were switched at birth 38 years ago, leading their families to sue Unity Medical Center in North Dakota. The lawsuit all...

item.title: Dorset Council Faces Conflict Over Winfrith Nuclear Site Restoration Plans
item.description: The article discusses the proposed restoration of the former Winfrith nuclear site to natural heathland, a plan by the Nuclear Decommissioning Agency that has sparked controversy with neighboring Inno...

item.title: Java House Foundation Launches 35 Fully Funded Scholarships for Young Kenyans in Coffee Sector
item.description: Java House Foundation, in partnership with Dedan Kimathi University of Technology (DeKUT), has launched a scholarship program offering 35 fully funded opportunities for Kenyan youths aged 18-22. The i...

item.title: Grenade Attack During BARMM Election Gun Ban Injures Five
item.description: A grenade attack injured five people on the third day of the election gun ban for the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) parliamentary elections, which are scheduled for September...

item.title: Community warned about child protection and infrastructure development

item.title: CTD Conducts Successful Intelligence Operation in Dera Ismail Khan, Kills Wanted Terrorist

item.title: Multiple Injuries Reported After Stage 12 Tour de France Crash
item.description: Following a high-speed crash during the final kilometer of stage 12 of the Tour de France, multiple riders sustained injuries, with several forced to withdraw from the race. The incident occurred as r...

item.title: Neil Rimer Advocates for Wealth Redistribution in Tech Amid Rising AI Billionaire Wealth
item.description: The article explores Neil Rimer's perspective on wealth concentration within the tech industry, particularly among AI billionaires. As a prominent investor at Index Ventures, Rimer highlights concerns...

item.title: William Troost-Ekong Plans to Influence Football Governance from Boardroom
item.description: Former Nigerian captain William Troost-Ekong, who ended his international career in 2025 after 83 caps, has revealed his intention to transition into football administration post-retirement. Ekong, wh...

item.title: A Novella's Bold Exploration of Resistance Against Caste and Patriarchy
item.description: Abha Purbey's novella 'Gulabiya', translated from the Angika language into English, explores themes of personal rebellion against caste and patriarchy. The story follows Gulabiya, a young farm laborer...
